title: poa glauca subsp. altaica
intro: Poa glauca subsp. altaica is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic acids in its extracts. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In traditional medicine, Poa glauca subsp. altaica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and topical creams for its medicinal and therapeutic applications.
